name: Italian Meringue Frosting
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 3-cup saucepan, combine the water and 2/3 cup sugar. Place over low heat to dissolve the sugar.
2. Increase the heat to medium-high and boil until a candy thermometer reads 235 degrees (wash down any sugar crystals with a brush dipped in cold water).
3. Near the end of boiling time, whip the egg whites with the cream of tartar until soft peaks form.
4. Add the 2 tablespoons sugar and continue to whip until the whites are stiff but not dry.
5. With the mixer running, pour the syrup onto the whipped whites. Continue to whip on medium speed for about 3 more minutes.
6. The mixture will thicken, cool, and form glossy, stiff peaks. Add the vanilla.
7. Cool to room temperature, about 8 to 10 minutes, then frost the cake immediately.
